_article: XXXIX.7 — Hydrology Graph is a first-class compiled artifact. XXXIX.2 — rivers continue to erode.
_description: Schema for the COMPILED Hydrology Graph — the water-flow graph where nodes are Rivers, Springs, Lakes, Seas, Floodplains, and Chokepoints (bridges/fords/ferries), and edges are flow relationships (drains_into, fed_by_spring, floods). The river_erosion_momentum reads and advances this graph; the travel_graph queries it for chokepoint passability; the settlement_graph queries it for flood risk.

nodes.node_id: string — entity id (River | Spring | Lake | Sea | Floodplain | Bridge | Ford | Ferry)
nodes.node_type: enum — river | spring | lake | sea | floodplain | bridge | ford | ferry

flow_state.flow_class: enum — trickle | stream | river | torrent | seasonal | frozen
flow_state.flow_force: float — volume * speed (drives erosion and flood)
flow_state.navigability: enum — none | small_craft | medium | large | seasonal

edges.type: enum — drains_into | fed_by_spring | floods | crosses (river crosses road — creates a chokepoint)
edges.properties: object — e.g. a 'crosses' edge references the bridge/ford/ferry entity and its integrity; a 'floods' edge references the floodplain and its fertility_gain_per_flood

indices.by_river: map<river_id, [edge_id]> — what does this river drain into / flood / cross
indices.by_chokepoint: map<chokepoint_id, crossing_edge> — for travel_graph chokepoint queries

queries_supported: drainage_hierarchy(river_id) -> [river_id | sea_id] — where does this water go
flood_risk_at(x, z) -> enum — for settlement flood risk
chokepoint_state(bridge_id | ford_id | ferry_id) -> {passable: bool, integrity: float} — for travel_graph
spirit_mineral_sites() -> [{bend_id, vein_coupling, deposition_rate}] — for foraging opportunities

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — River, Spring, Lake, Sea, Floodplain, Bridge/Ford/Ferry entities + drains_into/fed_by_spring/floods/crosses relationships
